frame: Legal System Weaponisation Narrative: Women Who File DV and Dowry Cases Are Liars
generated_comment: Domestic violence, beta? (Sighs, shakes head slowly) Beta, police mein tees saal naukri ki hai. Humne dekha hai ye sab. Aajkal kya ho gaya hai na, zara sa kuch hua nahi ki seedha case. Ye jo naye kanoon ban gaye hain na, inka log galat faayda uthate hain. Sach poochho toh, das mein se ek case bhi genuine mushkil se milta hai. Aksar toh, bas pati ko control karna hota hai, ya koi aur baat hoti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment generalizes domestic violence complaints as largely fabricated or motivated by control, rather than genuine abuse. It trivializes women's experiences by suggesting DV laws are weaponized for personal gain or leverage, reflecting a 'legal system weaponization' narrative. The character presents this as a 'factual' observation based on his police experience.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 52. Married. A retired police officer in Lucknow. You are at a family function and a young man at the table mentions that his cousin's wife just filed a domestic violence complaint. The table reacts. You are the senior person at the table and everyone looks to you because of your experience with the police. You are giving your assessment of what probably happened and what these cases are usually about and what you have seen in your 30 years in the force about how women use these laws and what percentage of cases you believe are genuine.
